content.problemStatement:
There are nnn red balls kept on the positive XXX axis, and mmm blue balls kept on the positive YYY axis. You are given the positions of the balls. For each iii from 111 to nnn, the iii-th red ball has the coordinates (xi,0)(x_i, 0)(xiвЂ‹,0), where xix_ixiвЂ‹ is a positive integer. For each iii from 111 to mmm, the iii-th blue ball has the coordinates (0,yi)(0, y_i)(0,yiвЂ‹), where yi y_iyiвЂ‹ is a positive integer.

It is given that all xix_ixiвЂ‹ are distinct. Also, all yiy_iyiвЂ‹ are distinct.

At the time t=0t= 0t=0, for each iii from 111 to nnn, the ithi^{\text{th}}ith red ball is thrown towards positive YYY axis with a speed of uiu_iuiвЂ‹( that is, with velocity vector (0,ui)(0, u_i)(0,uiвЂ‹)). Simultaneously (at time t=0t = 0t=0), for each iii from 111 to mmm, the ithi^{\text{th}}ith blue ball is thrown towards positive XXX axis with a speed of viv_iviвЂ‹ (that is, with velocity vector (vi,0)(v_i, 0)(viвЂ‹,0)).

Two balls are said to collide if they are at the same position at the same time. When two balls collide, they disappear, and hence no longer collide with other balls. (See sample examples for clarification).

Find the total number of collisions the balls will have.
constraints: for all 1в‰¤i<jв‰¤n,xiв‰ xj1 \le i \lt j \le n, x_i \neq x_j1в‰¤i<jв‰¤n,xiвЂ‹оЂ =xjвЂ‹
for all 1в‰¤i<jв‰¤m,yiв‰ yj1 \le i \lt j \le m, y_i \neq y_j1в‰¤i<jв‰¤m,yiвЂ‹оЂ =yjвЂ‹
content.inputFormat:
First line contains nnn and mmm, the number of red balls, and the number of blue balls, respectively.
ithi^{\text{th}}ith  of the next nnn lines contains two space separated integers xix_ixiвЂ‹ and uiu_iuiвЂ‹, the position and speed of the ithi^{\text{th}}ith  red ball respectively
ithi^{\text{th}}ith  of the next mmm lines contains two space separated integers yiy_iyiвЂ‹ and viv_iviвЂ‹, the position and speed of the ithi^{\text{th}}ith  blue ball respectively

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The balls collide at t = 1, at the coordinates (1, 2).

Explanation:
Example case 2: The red ball and the second blue ball collide at time 0.5 at coordinates (1, 1). Note that the first blue ball would have collided with the red ball at t = 1 (like in sample input # 1), if the second blue ball wasn't present. But since the red ball disappears at t = 0.5, its collision with first blue ball doesn't happen. The total number of collisions is 1.
